title: Complaint of a Prisoner against Fortune
summary: CME00094 is the checked-in repository copy of the University of Michigan encoding of the twenty-one-stanza, 147-line version of the anonymous Complaint of a Prisoner against Fortune printed from British Library Harley MS 7333 by Eleanor Prescott Hammond in 1909. The encoding contains Hammond's poem on printed pp. 484-490 but not her introduction on pp. 481-483. Hammond transcribed Harley 7333, ff. 30va-31ra, and collated the related twenty-stanza copies in Harley MS 2251 and Add MS 34360. DIMEV v1.0 documents all three witnesses. A separate 2005 METS edition uses Harley 2251 as its base and is therefore supporting rather than upstream of CME00094. Complete copies of Anglia volume 32, DIMEV v1.0, the complete METS book PDF, and the current British Library digitisation price list are cached. The BL catalog's legacy digital-image links currently report the three manuscript objects unavailable, so no manuscript facsimile is claimed as downloaded.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00094
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00094
entities.description: Public electronic text of Hammond's twenty-one-stanza Harley 7333 version, printed pp. 484-490. It retains verse groups, marginal speakers, printed notes, page and manuscript-folio milestones, and italic typography while excluding the article introduction on pp. 481-483.

entities.label: Eleanor Prescott Hammond, 'Lament of a Prisoner Against Fortune' (1909)
entities.description: Hammond's Anglia article introduces three related manuscript copies on pp. 481-483 and prints the twenty-one-stanza Harley 7333 text on pp. 484-490 with variants from Harley 2251 and Add 34360. She rejects a secure attribution and treats the author as unknown.

bibliographic.citation: Eleanor Prescott Hammond, 'Lament of a Prisoner Against Fortune,' Anglia 32 (1909): 481-490.
bibliographic.title: Lament of a Prisoner Against Fortune

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Anglia volume 32
entities.description: A 532-page PDF scan of the complete 1909 serial volume. Hammond's article is PDF pp. 491-500; the poem encoded as CME00094 is PDF pp. 494-500.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete bound-volume PDF with OCR and metadata derivatives

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 7333
entities.description: Hammond's direct base witness. This large parchment miscellany was produced in Leicester in the second half of the fifteenth century and is associated with John Shirley. The independent twenty-one-stanza Complaint occupies ff. 30va-31ra and has its own rubric and explicit.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: Second half of the fifteenth century

physical_description.format: Large codex, two columns, Gothic cursive
notes: The current catalog associates production with Leicester, John Shirley, and possibly St Mary de Pratis.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2251
entities.description: A paper manuscript copied in London by the Hammond Scribe, perhaps John Multon, for John Vale. Its twenty-stanza Complaint on current ff. 271r-273r (medieval fols. 298-300) follows Chaucer's Complaint to His Purse without an independent break and was collated by Hammond.

notes: The older pen foliation reaches 322 with omissions; DIMEV and the catalog preserve both current and medieval locators.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 34360
entities.description: A mid- to late-fifteenth-century paper miscellany copied in London by the Hammond Scribe. Its twenty-stanza Complaint on ff. 19r-21v follows Chaucer's Complaint to His Purse as if continuous and was collated by Hammond.

notes: The current top-level catalog identifies England as origin; the component and METS records associate the Hammond Scribe with London.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 record 1432 (IMEV/NIMEV 860)
entities.description: Versioned scholarly census for 'Fortune alas alas what have I guilt,' describing the anonymous Complaint as twenty or twenty-one rhyme-royal stanzas and recording all three witnesses with loci, stanza counts, rubric and explicit.

entities.label: Mooney and Arn, The Kingis Quair and Other Prison Poems (2005)
entities.description: A later scholarly edition by Linne R. Mooney and Mary-Jo Arn. Its Complaint text uses Harley 2251 as base and collates Add 34360 and Harley 7333. It is a valuable parallel edition and study, but it is not upstream of the 1909 Hammond text encoded by CME00094.

bibliographic.citation: Linne R. Mooney and Mary-Jo Arn, eds., The Kingis Quair and Other Prison Poems (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2005), 173-184.
bibliographic.title: The Kingis Quair and Other Prison Poems

rights.component: Underlying medieval textual work and witness text
rights.basis: The anonymous medieval work and all surviving witnesses were created centuries before modern copyright. This does not govern modern photographs, transcriptions, editions, interfaces, or provider terms.

rights.component: U-M CME digital text and item materials
rights.basis: The U-M CME collection applies the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement to its public materials.
notes: This jurisdiction-specific statement must not be generalized to BL manuscript photographs or other countries.

rights.component: Hammond's 1909 article, transcription, and editorial apparatus
rights.basis: The article was published in 1909, before the current United States public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan and derivative files
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata reports possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT in the United States and no visible notice for the 1909 volume, but supplies no explicit worldwide reuse license for every scan derivative.

rights.component: Any future British Library photographs or supplied digital facsimile of Harley MS 7333
rights.basis: No public image object or supplied reproduction was available to inspect. BL requires publication enquiries separately from digitisation ordering.

rights.component: British Library photographs or supplied digital facsimile of Harley MS 2251
rights.basis: The legacy digital object is unavailable and no item-specific image license or supplied reproduction was available to inspect.

rights.component: British Library photographs or supplied digital facsimile of Add MS 34360
rights.basis: The legacy digital object is unavailable and no item-specific image license or supplied reproduction was available to inspect.

rights.component: DIMEV version 1.0 data and source release
rights.basis: The DIMEV v1.0 release declares Creative Commons Attribution 4.0.

rights.component: METS web edition and complete downloadable print-edition PDF
rights.basis: METS states that its digital editions are for individual use and directs users to the Executive Director for permission to reprint digital text; the downloadable book PDF is not presented as openly licensed.

rights.component: METS XML files where separately provided
rights.basis: The METS copyright page separately identifies XML files under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0; that license is not generalized to the PDF or web interface.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hammond's complete article on printed pp. 481-490
editorial_practices.representation: Only the twenty-one-stanza edited poem on printed pp. 484-490
editorial_practices.description: The encoding excludes Hammond's title, description of the three witnesses, Shirley-context argument, authorship discussion, and comparison with Chaucer's Complaint to His Purse.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Twenty-one-stanza Harley 7333 base text and two related twenty-stanza copies
editorial_practices.representation: Harley 7333 main text with footnoted variants and local bracketed supplies informed by the other manuscripts
editorial_practices.description: Hammond's text is not a diplomatic rendering of one codex: Harley 7333 remains the base, while Harley 2251 and Add 34360 provide collateral readings and supplies.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hammond's italic typography can mark editorial expansions or other distinctions
editorial_practices.representation: The XML preserves italic spans without reconstructing the original manuscript signs
editorial_practices.description: Italic text in CME00094 is evidence for Hammond's printed typography, not direct evidence for the shape of a manuscript abbreviation. The unavailable manuscript images are needed to identify original signs securely.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three related witnesses: Harley 7333 with twenty-one stanzas, Harley 2251 and Add 34360 with twenty
editorial_practices.representation: Hammond prints the complete Harley 7333 version, including the stanza absent from the other two
editorial_practices.description: The 147-line CME extent belongs to the Harley 7333 version; it must not be silently replaced by the 140-line extent of the later METS edition's Harley 2251 base.

open_questions.question: Who composed the Complaint, and when and where was it composed?
next_steps: Treat the poem as anonymous rather than adopting rejected Lydgate, Usk, Ashby, Roos, or Suffolk proposals.
Consult later work-specific scholarship for a date narrower than the fifteenth century and for composition place.
Keep manuscript production places separate from composition place.

open_questions.question: What exact textual relationship connects the Shirley-associated Harley 7333 copy with the two Hammond-Scribe copies?
next_steps: Consult a modern stemmatic or codicological study of the three witnesses.
Do not invent a lost Shirley exemplar as a graph entity without direct evidence.
Preserve the twenty-one- versus twenty-stanza version distinction.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library restore or supply complete color digital facsimiles of all three manuscripts with written reuse terms?
next_steps: Ask about the unavailable legacy ARKs before ordering new photography.
Request each whole manuscript, including covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, edges if offered, and every leaf, while separately mapping the Complaint loci.
Ask explicitly about Git redistribution and commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand use before publishing supplied images.
Obtain a shelfmark-specific quote rather than extrapolating from the general rate card.

open_questions.question: What reuse permission applies to the complete METS book PDF as distinct from the separately licensed XML?
next_steps: Contact the METS Executive Director before redistributing the PDF or modern edited text.
Keep CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 limited to the XML files named by the provider.
Use the cached PDF only for private source comparison until permission is clarified.

review_notes: The primary transmission path is repository XML to U-M encoding to Hammond's 1909 Harley 7333 edition to Harley MS 7333; Harley 2251 and Add 34360 are collateral witnesses used in Hammond's collation.
The twenty-one-stanza, 147-line extent belongs to the Harley 7333 version encoded by CME00094. The two other witnesses and the later METS base text have twenty stanzas and 140 lines.
The later METS edition is supporting scholarship, not an upstream source of the 1909-based encoding.
The inherited Lydgate attribution in the Add 34360 catalog is not adopted; Hammond and the modern METS edition treat authorship as unknown.

No BL manuscript imagery is cached because all three legacy objects were unavailable at review time. The manifest records that dated service state, request routes, and whole-object reproduction preference without claiming the manuscripts were never digitized.
All public supporting files used were downloaded complete where possible and hard-linked into the main checkout's ignored source cache.
